business_function: Stores and manages student identity, academic profile, contact, and enrollment-related information.
primary_data_processed: Student identity, contact details, academic profile, enrollment status
system_dependency: Academic Database; Admin Portal; Identity and Access Management
availability_requirement: High - needed for daily academic administration and student services
key_risk_exposure: Unauthorized access, excessive privilege, personal data exposure, inaccurate student records
related_grc_area: Access Management; Data Privacy; Data Quality
source_reference: NIST CSF ID.AM, PR.AA, PR.DS; NIST SP 800-53 AC, IA; ISO 27001 CIA; Indonesia PDP Law
notes: Primary personal data asset for this simulated assessment.

business_function: Supports course enrollment, class selection, registration schedule, and registration status visibility.
primary_data_processed: Course selection, registration status, student enrollment records
system_dependency: Academic Database; Admin Portal; Reporting Dashboard
availability_requirement: High - especially during course registration periods
key_risk_exposure: Service unavailability, registration errors, unauthorized schedule changes, business process disruption
related_grc_area: Business Continuity; Change Management; Access Management
source_reference: NIST CSF ID.AM, PR.PS, RC.RP; NIST SP 800-53 CP, CM, AC; COBIT governance concepts
notes: Critical operational asset due to time-sensitive academic registration processes.

business_function: Displays payment or tuition-related status to authorized academic and administrative users.
primary_data_processed: Payment status, billing visibility, student financial status indicators
system_dependency: Academic Database; Admin Portal; Reporting Dashboard
availability_requirement: Medium - important for service eligibility checks and administrative decisions
key_risk_exposure: Sensitive information exposure, incorrect payment status, unauthorized data viewing, integrity issues
related_grc_area: Data Privacy; Data Integrity; Access Management
source_reference: NIST CSF PR.AA, PR.DS; NIST SP 800-53 AC, IA, SI; ISO 27001 confidentiality/integrity; Indonesia PDP Law
notes: Sensitive status visibility; does not simulate full payment processing.

business_function: Handles academic document request submission, processing, approval, and completion status.
primary_data_processed: Document request details, requestor information, approval status, service notes
system_dependency: Academic Database; Admin Portal; Incident Ticketing Log
availability_requirement: Medium - needed for student service continuity
key_risk_exposure: Incomplete request evidence, unclear workflow ownership, delayed processing, inaccurate status tracking
related_grc_area: Workflow Control; Evidence Tracking; Service Monitoring
source_reference: NIST CSF ID.AM, PR.PS; NIST SP 800-53 CM, PL; COBIT governance concepts
notes: Useful for showing evidence and workflow accountability concepts.

business_function: Provides staff and administrative users with access to manage academic records, requests, and operational information.
primary_data_processed: Administrative actions, user updates, workflow approvals, data changes
system_dependency: Identity and Access Management; Academic Database; Logging & Monitoring
availability_requirement: High - required for operational support and service administration
key_risk_exposure: Privileged access misuse, lack of access review, insufficient segregation of duties, weak activity monitoring
related_grc_area: Privileged Access Management; Logging & Monitoring; Data Privacy
source_reference: NIST CSF PR.AA, DE.CM; NIST SP 800-53 AC, AU, IA; CIS Controls Account Management; Indonesia PDP Law
notes: High-priority asset for later access management risks and controls.

business_function: Stores core data used by the academic services system, including student, registration, request, and status records.
primary_data_processed: Student records, registration data, payment status indicators, service request data
system_dependency: Backup Storage; Admin Portal; Reporting Dashboard; Logging & Monitoring
availability_requirement: High - system modules depend on database availability and integrity
key_risk_exposure: Data loss, unauthorized database access, integrity failure, backup failure, insufficient monitoring
related_grc_area: Data Protection; Backup & Recovery; Logging & Monitoring; Access Management
source_reference: NIST CSF PR.DS, DE.CM, RC.RP; NIST SP 800-53 AC, AU, CP, SC; ISO 27001 CIA; Indonesia PDP Law
notes: Central asset with high confidentiality, integrity, and availability relevance.

business_function: Provides management and operational reporting for academic service status, requests, and performance indicators.
primary_data_processed: Aggregated operational metrics, service status, academic reporting indicators
system_dependency: Academic Database; Data Quality Validation; User Access Controls
availability_requirement: Medium - supports monitoring and management decisions
key_risk_exposure: Incorrect reporting, incomplete data, weak data quality validation, inappropriate report access
related_grc_area: Data Quality; Reporting Governance; Access Management
source_reference: NIST CSF ID.AM, PR.DS; NIST SP 800-53 SI, AC; COBIT information quality concepts
notes: Important for linking data quality to management reporting risk.

business_function: Stores backup copies required for system restoration and service continuity.
primary_data_processed: Backup files, restoration points, database/system snapshots
system_dependency: Academic Database; Recovery Procedure; Backup Monitoring
availability_requirement: High - required for recovery from data loss or service disruption
key_risk_exposure: Backup restoration failure, missing restore testing, backup data exposure, recovery delay
related_grc_area: Backup & Recovery; Business Continuity; Data Protection
source_reference: NIST CSF RC.RP, PR.DS; NIST SP 800-53 CP, SC; ISO 27001 availability concepts; CIS Data Recovery
notes: Key asset for recovery readiness and business continuity risk.

asset_name: Identity and Access Management / User Directory
business_function: Manages user accounts, authentication, user roles, and access provisioning for academic services.
primary_data_processed: User account records, role assignments, authentication and access metadata
system_dependency: Admin Portal; Student Profile Module; Logging & Monitoring
availability_requirement: High - access to critical modules depends on identity services
key_risk_exposure: Dormant accounts, excessive privileges, weak access review, unauthorized account provisioning
related_grc_area: Access Management; Privileged Access; Governance Accountability
source_reference: NIST CSF PR.AA; NIST SP 800-53 AC, IA; CIS Account Management; COBIT governance concepts
notes: Used later for risks around access review and privileged access.

business_function: Stores change requests, approvals, UAT results, release notes, and deployment decisions.
primary_data_processed: Change request forms, UAT evidence, approval records, release notes
system_dependency: Admin Portal; Developer Handoff; UAT Documentation
availability_requirement: Medium - needed for change traceability and audit readiness
key_risk_exposure: Undocumented changes, missing UAT evidence, unclear approval trail, production errors
related_grc_area: Change Management; Evidence Tracking; Control Assessment
source_reference: NIST CSF PR.PS; NIST SP 800-53 CM, SA; COBIT change management concepts
notes: Primary asset for change management and audit evidence risks.

business_function: Records incidents, service issues, severity, escalation, resolution notes, and closure status.
primary_data_processed: Incident tickets, severity classification, owner assignment, resolution evidence
system_dependency: Admin Portal; Logging & Monitoring; Incident Response Procedure
availability_requirement: High - supports incident response and service recovery monitoring
key_risk_exposure: Incomplete escalation, delayed response, missing incident evidence, inconsistent severity classification
related_grc_area: Incident Handling; Evidence Tracking; Service Recovery
source_reference: NIST CSF RS.MA, RS.AN, RS.CO; NIST SP 800-53 IR; CIS Incident Response Management
notes: Important for response and recovery assessment in later steps.

business_function: Supports coordination with external service provider for system support, incident escalation, and service requests.
primary_data_processed: Support tickets, service requests, SLA notes, issue updates, vendor communication
system_dependency: Incident Ticketing Log; Change Request Repository; Service Level Agreement
availability_requirement: Medium - needed for support coordination and escalation
key_risk_exposure: Vendor dependency, unclear SLA evidence, delayed escalation, third-party access concerns
related_grc_area: Vendor Dependency; Supply Chain Risk; Incident Handling
source_reference: NIST CSF GV.SC; NIST SP 800-53 SR, SA; COBIT vendor/service management concepts
notes: Used later for supplier and third-party risk mapping.
